1: The Lord spoke to Moses from the Tent of Meeting.
He said to Moses, ‘Speak to Israel's people. Tell them this. When a person gives an animal to the Lord, it must be a cow, a sheep or a goat.

A person may want to give a burnt offering to the Lord. That offering may be a bull that he takes from among his animals. It must be perfect. The person must bring the bull to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, so that the Lord will accept his gift.
Then he must put his hand on the animal's head. The Lord will accept the animal's death to pay for the person's sins.

The person who brings the offering must kill the young bull in front of the Lord. Aaron's sons, the priests, will take the blood. They will throw it onto the sides of the altar which is near the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
The person must remove the skin from the dead animal. Then he must cut the meat into pieces.
Aaron's sons will light a fire on the altar. They will put pieces of wood on it.
Then Aaron's sons will put the pieces of meat onto the wood that is burning. They will put the head and the fat on the fire with the other meat.

The person who brings the offering must use water to wash the legs and the inside parts of the animal. Then the priest will burn the whole animal on the altar. The smell of the burnt offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
A person may want to give a sheep or a goat as a burnt offering to the Lord. His offering must be a perfect male animal.
He must kill it in front of the Lord, at the north side of the altar. Aaron's sons, the priests, will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar.
The person who brings the offering must cut the animal into pieces. The priests will take the pieces of meat, the head and the fat. They will put them on the wood which is burning on the altar.

The person who brings the offering must use water to wash the legs and the inside parts of the animal. Then the priest will burn the whole animal on the altar. The smell of the burnt offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
A person may want to give a bird as a burnt offering to the Lord. His offering must be a dove or a young pigeon.
The priest will take the bird to the altar. He will tear off the head and he will burn it on the altar. He will pour the bird's blood onto the side of the altar.
Then the priest will remove the part of the bird where it keeps its food, and the food in it. He will throw it on the east side of the altar, where the ashes are.

He will take hold of the bird's wings and he will tear open the body. But he must not tear it completely into two pieces. He will burn all of it on the wood that is burning on the altar. The smell of the burnt offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
2: A person may want to give a grain offering to the Lord. He must choose the best flour for his offering. He must pour olive oil on the flour and he must put incense on it.

He must take it to Aaron's sons, the priests. The priest will take some of the flour and its olive oil in his hand. He will also take all the incense. He will burn this part of the offering on the altar as a gift to the Lord. The smell of the offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
The part of the grain offering that remains will belong to Aaron and his sons. It is a very holy part of the gifts that people offer to the Lord.

If you bake your grain in an oven before you offer it, this is what you must do. You must use the best flour. Do not use any yeast. You may mix olive oil with the flour to make a loaf. Or you may make biscuits and put olive oil on them.
You may choose to cook a cake on a flat plate. If so, mix some olive oil with the best flour but do not use any yeast.
You must break the cake into pieces and then pour olive oil on it. It is a grain offering.
If you cook the grain in a pot, you must use the best flour. Cook it in olive oil.
Use these things to make your grain offering for the Lord. Then you must take it to the priest. He will take it to the altar.
He will take a part of the grain offering and he will burn it in the fire on the altar. The smell of this offering will give pleasure to the Lord.
The part of the grain offering that remains will belong to Aaron and his sons. It is a very holy part of the gifts that people offer to the Lord.
You must not use yeast in any grain offering that you give to the Lord. You must not give any yeast or honey to the priests for them to burn as a gift to the Lord.
You may offer yeast or honey to the Lord as the first part of your harvest. But you must not burn them on the altar to make a sweet smell that gives pleasure to the Lord.
You must put salt on all your grain offerings. Salt is a mark of God's covenant with you, his people. So do not forget to put salt on all your grain offerings.
You may want to use the first crops of your harvest to make a grain offering to the Lord. If so, you must break the new grains into small pieces. Then cook them on a fire.
It is a grain offering, so you must put olive oil and incense on it.
The priest will take a part of the grain and olive oil, together with all the incense. He will burn that part as a gift to the Lord.
3: A person might want to give a friendship offering to the Lord. He may choose a bull or a cow from among his animals. It must be perfect.
The person must put his hand on the animal's head. Then he must kill it at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Aaron's sons, the priests, will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar.
The person must offer some parts of the friendship offering as a gift to the Lord. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal.
He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver.

Aaron's sons will take these parts of the animal. They will put them on top of the burnt offering that is on the wood that burns on the altar. The smell of them while they are burning will give pleasure to the Lord.
A person may want to give a sheep or a goat as a friendship offering to the Lord. It must be a perfect animal, male or female.
If it is a young sheep, he must offer it as a gift to the Lord.
He must put his hand on its head. Then he must kill it at the front of the Tent of Meeting. Aaron's sons will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar.

The person must offer some parts of the friendship offering as a gift to the Lord. He must cut the fat part of the tail off the animal. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal.
He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver.
The priest will burn these parts on the altar as a gift of food to the Lord.
A person may give a goat as his offering to the Lord.
The person must put his hand on the goat's head. Then he must kill it at the front of the Tent of Meeting. Then Aaron's sons will throw the blood onto the sides of the altar.
The person must offer some parts of his offering as a gift to the Lord. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal.
He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver.
The priest will burn these parts on the altar as a gift of food. The smell of them while they are burning will give pleasure to the Lord. All the fat from these offerings belongs to the Lord.
You must never eat any fat or any blood yourselves. This is a rule that you and your descendants must always obey, in all the places where you live.’
4: The Lord said to Moses,

‘Say to Israel's people, “Someone may do a wrong thing when he did not want to do it. He may do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands, so it is a sin. This is what that person must do.

The person who does a wrong thing might be the leader of the priests. If that is so, his sin will cause the people to be guilty. He must bring a perfect young bull as an offering to the Lord. That will be his sin offering.
He must bring the young bull to the Lord at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must put his hand on the bull's head. Then he must kill it in front of the Lord.
The leader of the priests must take some of the blood into the Tent of Meeting.
He must put his finger in the blood. Then he must shake the blood seven times towards the curtain of the Lord 's Most Holy Place.

He must also put some of the blood on the horns of the incense altar. That altar is in the Lord 's Holy Place, inside the Tent of Meeting. He must pour all the bull's blood that remains onto the ground in front of the great altar. That is the altar for burnt offerings which is outside the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
Then the leader of the priests must remove all the fat from the bull which is his sin offering. He must remove the fat that is around the inside parts of the animal.
He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is around them. And he must remove the bottom part of the liver.
That is the same as the priest does with a bull which is a friendship offering. The priest must burn all the fat on the altar which is for burnt offerings.
But this is what the priest must do with all the other parts of the bull. He must take its skin, its meat, its head and its legs, as well as its inside parts and the food in its stomach.
He must take all those parts to a clean place which is outside the camp. He must burn them on a fire of wood on the heap of ashes.

The whole group of Israel's people might do a wrong thing when they did not want to do it. They might do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands. They are guilty even if they did not know about it.
When they realize that they have done a sin, they must offer a young bull to the Lord. That will be their sin offering. They must bring it to the Tent of Meeting.
The leaders of the people must put their hands on the bull's head. Then one of them must kill it in front of the Lord.
The leader of the priests must take some of the bull's blood into the Tent of Meeting.
He must put his finger in the blood. Then he must shake the blood seven times towards the curtain of the Lord 's Most Holy Place.

He must also put some of the blood on the horns of the incense altar. That altar is in the Lord 's Holy Place, inside the Tent of Meeting. He must pour all the bull's blood that remains onto the ground in front of the great altar. That is the altar for burnt offerings which is outside the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
Then the priest must remove all the fat from the dead animal. He will burn all the fat on the altar for burnt offerings.

He will do with the other parts of this bull the same things that he did with his own sin offering. This is how the priest will make the people clean from their sins. God will forgive them when the priest does this.
The priest must take the other parts of the bull outside the camp. He must burn them as he did with the first bull. This is the sin offering for the whole group of Israel's people.
A leader might do a wrong thing when he did not want to do it. He might do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands, so he is guilty.
When he realizes that he has done a sin, he must bring a perfect male goat as an offering to the Lord.
He must put his hand on the goat's head. Then he must kill it in the place where they kill animals for burnt offerings to the Lord. It is a sin offering for the leader.

The priest must put his finger in the goat's blood. He will put some of it on the horns of the altar which is for burnt offerings. He must pour the blood that remains onto the ground in front of that altar.

Then the priest must remove all the fat from the goat. He must burn the fat on the altar, as he does for the sacrifices which are friendship offerings. This is how the priest will make the leader clean from his sin. God will forgive the leader when the priest does this.

One of the ordinary people might do a wrong thing when he did not want to do it. He might do something that is against one of the Lord 's commands. People should always obey those commands, so he is guilty.
When he realizes that he has done a sin, he should bring a perfect female goat as an offering to the Lord.
He must put his hand on the head of the goat which is his sin offering. Then he must kill it in the place where they kill animals for burnt offerings.

The priest must put his finger in the goat's blood. He will put some of it on the horns of the altar which is for burnt offerings. He must pour the blood that remains onto the ground in front of that altar.

Then the priest must remove all the fat from the goat, as he did with the sacrifice for the friendship offering. He must burn the fat on the altar so that its smell gives pleasure to the Lord. This is how the priest will make the person clean from his sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this.
If the person chooses to offer a sheep as his sin offering, it must be a perfect female sheep.
He must put his hand on the head of the sheep which is his sin offering. He must then kill it at the place where they kill animals for burnt offerings.

The priest must put his finger in the sheep's blood. He will put some of it on the horns of the altar which is for burnt offerings. He must pour all the blood that remains onto the ground in front of the altar.

He must remove all the fat from the sheep. This is the same as they do with fat from sheep which are sacrifices for friendship offerings. The priest must then burn the fat on the altar. He will burn it on top of the other offerings that people give to the Lord. This is how the priest will make the person clean from their sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this.
5: Someone may have seen something happen, or they may know about it. A judge may command people to say anything that they know about what happened. If the person refuses to tell what he knows about it, he is guilty of a sin. He deserves punishment.

Someone may touch something that is unclean. It may be the dead body of a wild animal or a farm animal that is not clean. Or he may touch the dead body of an unclean animal that moves near to the ground. Even if the person does not realize what he has done, he has made himself unclean. He is guilty of a sin.
Someone may touch something that comes out from another person and it is unclean. The person may not know that he has touched it, but later he realizes what he has done. He is guilty of a sin.

Someone may not think carefully before he makes a promise. He may promise to do something that is good or something that is bad. Even if he does not know that he has made a careless promise, he is guilty when he realizes what he has done.
If someone becomes guilty for any of these reasons, he must say clearly that he has done a wrong thing.

He must bring a female sheep or a female goat for his sin offering. That will be the price to pay for his sin. The priest will offer the animal to the Lord as a sin offering. The Lord will accept the animal's death to pay for the person's sin.

The person may not have enough money to bring an animal as his sin offering. If so, he must pay for his sin with two doves or two young pigeons. He must bring them to the Lord. One bird will be a sin offering. The second bird will be a burnt offering.
He must take his birds to the priest. The priest will first offer to the Lord the bird that is for a sin offering. He must break the bird's neck, but he must not remove the head from the body.
He must shake some of the blood onto the side of the altar. Then he must pour the blood that remains onto the ground in front of the altar. That bird is a sin offering.

The priest must then offer the other bird as a burnt offering. He must do this in the way that the rules say he should. This is how the priest will make the person clean from their sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this.

The person may not have enough money to bring two doves or two young pigeons. If so, he must pay for his sin with a tenth of an ephah of the best flour. That will be his sin offering. He must not put any olive oil or incense on the flour, because it is a sin offering.

He must take it to the priest. The priest must take some of the flour in his hand. He will burn this on the altar as a gift to the Lord, together with the other gifts. The smell of the offering will give pleasure to the Lord. It is a sin offering to the Lord.

In this way the priest will make the person clean from any of these sins that he has done. God will forgive the person when the priest does this. The flour that remains will belong to the priest, as the grain offerings do.” ’

‘Someone may not obey the rules about the Lord 's holy things. He may not realize that he has done anything wrong, but he is guilty of a sin. He must bring a perfect male sheep as a guilt offering to the Lord, to pay for his sin. You must decide the value of the sheep in shekels of silver that they use in the holy place.

The person must also give money to pay for the holy thing which he spoiled. He must add one fifth more to its value. He must give the sheep and the money to the priest. The priest will offer the sheep as a guilt offering to make the person clean from their sin. God will forgive the person when the priest does this.
Someone may fail to obey one of the Lord 's commands. Even if he does not know that he has done this, he is guilty of a sin. He deserves punishment.

When he realizes what he has done, he must bring a perfect male sheep to the priest. That will be his guilt offering. You must decide the value of the sheep in shekels of silver that they use in the holy place. The priest will offer the sheep to the Lord to make the person clean from his mistake. He did a wrong thing but he did not realize it at the time. God will forgive the person when the priest does this.
The sheep is a guilt offering for that person. The person is guilty of a sin against the Lord.’
6: The Lord said to Moses,

‘Someone may cheat another person, which is a sin against the Lord. He may deceive other people in different ways. He may keep something that belongs to them. It may be something that they have given to him to take care of. He may have robbed them. He may cheat them in other ways.
He may find something that another person has lost but he keeps it for himself. He may tell lies about it. He may say that he does not have it. He may deceive people in other ways that people do.

When someone does any of these sins, he is guilty. He must give back everything that he has taken from other people. He may have cheated other people. He may have kept something that belongs to them. He may have found something that they have lost.

He may have told lies about something so that he keeps it for himself. On the day when he knows that he is guilty, he must give back everything to the proper owner. He must also add one fifth more to its value.
He must bring a perfect male sheep as his guilt offering to the Lord. You must decide its value in shekels of silver. He must give this guilt offering to the priest.
The priest will offer the sheep to the Lord to make the person clean from his sin. Whatever sin the person has done to make him guilty, God will forgive him when the priest does this.’
The Lord said to Moses,

‘Give this command to Aaron and his sons: “These are the rules about burnt offerings. The burnt offering must stay on the altar fire all night until the morning. The fire on the altar must continue to burn all night.

When the morning comes, the priest must dress himself in his linen robe. He must also wear linen trousers next to his skin. He must remove the ashes of the burnt offering that has burnt on the fire. He must put the ashes at the side of the altar.
Then he must dress himself in other clothes. He must carry the ashes outside the camp. He must put them in a special place that God has chosen.

The fire that is on the altar must always continue to burn. Every morning the priest must put more wood on the fire. He will put the animal for the burnt offering on top of the fire. He will also burn the fat of the friendship offerings on the fire.
The fire on the altar must always continue to burn. It must never stop burning.
These are the rules about grain offerings. Aaron's sons must take them in front of the altar to give them to the Lord.

A priest must take some of the pure flour and the olive oil in his hand. He must also take all the incense that is on the grain offering. He will burn this part of the offering on the altar as a gift to the Lord. The smell of the offering will give pleasure to the Lord.

Aaron and his sons may eat what remains of the friendship offerings. They must use it to make bread that has no yeast in it. Then they must eat it in a holy place, in the yard outside the Tent of Meeting.

The bread must not have any yeast in it. I have given this part of my offerings to the priests. It belongs to them. It is very holy, in the same way that the sin offerings and the guilt offerings are holy.
Any male descendant of Aaron may eat this part of the Lord 's offerings. This rule will be true for all time. Anyone who touches these offerings will be holy.” ’
The Lord said to Moses,

‘On the day when you anoint Aaron and his sons, they must bring an offering to the Lord. It must be a tenth of an ephah of the best flour. It will be a regular grain offering. They must bring half of it in the morning and half of it in the evening.
They must mix it carefully with olive oil and they must cook it on a flat plate. Then they must break it into pieces and offer it to the Lord. The smell of it will give pleasure to the Lord.

The priest who must prepare the offering is Aaron's son that they anoint to take his father's place. This rule will continue for Aaron's descendants for all time. The priest must completely burn the whole offering as a gift to the Lord.
Every grain offering that a priest gives belongs to the Lord completely. Nobody may eat any of it.’
The Lord said to Moses,

‘Tell Aaron and his sons, “These are the rules about sin offerings. They must kill the animal for a sin offering at the place where they kill the animals for burnt offerings. It is a very holy offering that they give to the Lord.
The priest who offers this sacrifice will eat his part of the animal. He must do that in a holy place, in the yard outside the Tent of Meeting.
If anyone touches the meat, they will be holy. If the blood splashes onto the clothes of a person, he must wash them in a holy place.

If a person uses a clay pot to cook the meat, he must then break the pot into pieces. If a person uses a metal pot to cook the meat, he must rub the pot very well to make it clean. Then he must wash it with clean water.
The meat is very holy. Only a male person who belongs to a priest's family may eat it.

But it is different if they take some blood from the sin offering into the Tent of Meeting. If they do that to make people clean from their sin, nobody may eat any meat from the offering. Instead, the priest must burn the animal completely in the fire.”
7: Tell Aaron, “These are the rules about guilt offerings. It is a most holy offering.
They must kill the animal for a guilt offering at the place where they kill the animals for burnt offerings. The priest must throw the blood onto the sides of the altar.
The person who brings the offering must offer all the fat from the animal. That includes the fat part of its tail and the fat that is around its inside parts.
He must remove the two kidneys and the fat that is on them. He must also remove the bottom part of the liver. He must offer these to the Lord.
Then the priest must burn all these parts of the animal on the altar as a gift to the Lord. It is a guilt offering.
Any male person who belongs to a priest's family may eat the meat that remains. They must eat it in a holy place because it is very holy.
These rules are the same for sin offerings and for guilt offerings. The meat belongs to the priest who offers the animal to make a person clean from their sin.
But it is different with burnt offerings. The skin of the animal that the priest burns on the altar belongs to that priest.
If people bring a grain offering that they have cooked in an oven or on a flat plate, that offering belongs to the priest who offers it to the Lord.
It is different if the grain offering has dry grain, or the person has mixed the grain with olive oil. The grain from those offerings belongs to all the sons of Aaron, in equal parts.
These are the rules about sacrifices for friendship offerings which people may offer to the Lord.

If they do it to thank the Lord, they must bring these things together with the animal for the sacrifice: flat loaves of bread that have olive oil mixed in the flour, flat biscuits that have olive oil on them, and round loaves that have olive oil mixed with the best flour. None of these may have any yeast in them.
For their offering, they must also bring loaves of bread that have yeast in the flour.
They must offer one of each kind of these grain offerings to the Lord. These belong to the priest who throws the blood on the side of the altar for the friendship offering.
The people must eat the meat of the animal for the friendship offering on the same day that they offer it. They must not keep any of the meat until the next day.

It is different if people bring a friendship offering because of a promise that they have made to the Lord. Or if they do it because they choose to make a gift to the Lord. These people may eat some of the meat on the day that they offer the sacrifice. They may also eat the meat that remains on the next day.
But if any meat still remains on the third day, they must burn it completely.

If people eat any of the meat on the third day, the Lord will not accept their offering. The meat has become unclean, so God will not bless them for their offering. Instead, he will punish anyone who eats it on the third day.
If any of the meat touches something that is unclean, nobody may eat it. Instead, they must burn it completely. But anyone who is clean may eat the meat that remains.
Anyone who has not made themselves clean must not eat the meat of the sacrifice for a friendship offering. If they do that, they will no longer belong with Israel's people.

You become unclean if you touch anything that is unclean from a human, or from an animal or any other disgusting thing. If that happens, you must not eat the meat of the sacrifice for the friendship offering which belongs to the Lord. Anyone who eats it will no longer belong with Israel's people.” ’
The Lord said to Moses,
‘Tell Israel's people, “You must not eat any fat from bulls, sheep or goats.
You may find the body of an animal that has died by itself, or wild animals have killed it. You must never eat the fat of that animal, but you may use the fat for any other purpose.
You must not eat the fat of an animal that you have offered to the Lord as a gift. Anyone who does that will no longer belong to Israel's people.
Wherever you live, you must not eat the blood of any birds or animals.
Anyone who eats any blood will no longer belong to Israel's people.” ’
The Lord said to Moses,
‘Tell Israel's people, “When anyone brings a sacrifice for a friendship offering to the Lord, they must bring part of it as a gift to the Lord.
They must carry it in their own hands to burn on the altar. They must bring the animal's fat and the meat around its ribs. They must lift these up as a special offering to the Lord.
The priest will then burn the fat on the altar. But the meat around the ribs will belong to Aaron and his sons.
The top part of the back right leg of the animal is a gift to the priest.
It will belong to the priest who offers the blood and the fat for the friendship offering.

I have taken these parts of the animals that the Israelites bring as their friendship offerings. Those are the ribs that are lifted up to me, and the top part of the right leg that is a gift to the priests. I have taken these parts from the Israelite people and I have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons. That will always be the part that belongs to them.” ’
The Lord gave this part of the Israelites' offerings to Aaron and to his descendants. This started on the day that they became priests to serve the Lord.
The Lord commanded the Israelites to do this on the day that Moses anointed them as priests. This part of the offerings would always belong to the priests.
These are the rules for burnt offerings, grain offerings, sin offerings, guilt offerings, friendship offerings and offerings for new priests.
The Lord gave these rules to Moses on Mount Sinai. On that day, the Lord commanded Israel's people to start making these offerings to him in the Sinai Desert.
8: The Lord said to Moses,

‘Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Also bring their special clothes and the olive oil to anoint them as priests. Bring the bull for the sin offering and the two male sheep for sacrifices. Bring the basket of flat bread with no yeast in it.
Tell all the people to come together in front of the Tent of Meeting.’
Moses did what the Lord had commanded him to do. All the Israelite people met at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.
Moses said to the people, ‘The Lord has commanded us to do this.’
Moses told Aaron and his sons to stand in front of the people. He washed them with water.
Moses put the special shirt on Aaron. He tied the long belt around Aaron. He put the priest's robe on him. Then he put the ephod on him, with its belt. He used the belt to tie the ephod on Aaron.
He put the breastpiece on Aaron, and he put the Urim and Thummim in its pocket.
Moses put the linen hat on Aaron's head. He fixed the thin gold plate on the front of the hat, as a holy crown. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do.
Moses took some of the special olive oil. He splashed it on the tabernacle and on everything in it. He did this to show that they belonged to God.
He splashed oil on the altar seven times. He put oil on the altar's tools, on the large bowl for water and on its base. He did this to show that they belonged to God.
Then Moses poured some of the oil on Aaron's head to anoint him. This made Aaron ready to serve God as his priest.

Then Moses told Aaron's sons to come forward. He put a robe on each of them. He tied long cloth belts around them. He put cloth hats on their heads. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do.
Then Moses brought the bull for the sin offering. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the bull's head.

He killed the bull. Moses took some of the blood on his fingers. He put the blood on the four horns of the altar to make the altar clean. He poured out the blood that remained on the ground in front of the altar. In this way, he made the altar holy. It was now ready to make sacrifices for sin offerings.

Moses removed the fat that was around the inside parts of the bull. He removed the bottom part of its liver, as well as its two kidneys and the fat around them. He burned all these parts on the altar.
But Moses took outside the camp the parts of the bull that remained. There he completely burned its skin, its meat and the food in its stomach. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do.
Moses then brought one of the male sheep to be a burnt offering. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the sheep's head.
He killed the sheep. Moses splashed its blood onto the sides of the altar.
He cut the sheep into pieces. He burned its head, the pieces of meat and the fat on the altar.

He used water to wash its inside parts and its legs. He burned the whole sheep on the altar as a burnt offering to the Lord. The smell of this gift while it was burning gave pleasure to the Lord. Moses did this as the Lord had commanded him to do.
Moses then brought the other male sheep to be a sacrifice for new priests. Aaron and his sons put their hands on the sheep's head.
He killed the sheep. Moses took some of its blood. He put it on Aaron's right ear, on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.

Then Moses told Aaron's sons to come forward. He put blood on their right ears, on the thumbs of their right hands and on the toes of their right feet. Then he splashed the blood that remained onto the sides of the altar.

Moses took the fat part of the sheep's tail and the fat around its inside parts. He also took the bottom part of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat. With all the sheep's fat, he took the top part of its right back leg.

Moses took one round loaf of bread from the basket of flat bread that belonged to the Lord. He also took one loaf that had olive oil mixed in the flour. And he took one flat biscuit. He put them on top of the fat parts of the sheep and its right leg.
Then Moses put all of them on the hands of Aaron and his sons. They lifted them up as a special offering to the Lord.

Moses took them from their hands and he burned them on the altar, on top of the burnt offering. This was an offering for new priests. The smell of this offering while it was burning gave pleasure to the Lord.
Then Moses took the meat that was around the sheep's ribs. He lifted it up as a special offering to the Lord. This part belonged to Moses, as the Lord had commanded him.

Moses took some of the oil and some of the blood from the altar. He shook them on Aaron and his clothes. He also shook it on Aaron's sons and on their clothes. In this way he made them holy and ready to serve the Lord.

Moses said to Aaron and to his sons, ‘Cook the meat that remains at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. Eat the meat there, with the bread that is in the basket of bread for the offering. That is what the Lord has commanded you to do.
Completely burn any meat and bread that remain.
You must stay at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting for seven days. That is the time to prepare you to serve God as priests. You must not leave until the seven days have finished.
The things that we have done today are what the Lord has commanded us to do. We have done it to make you clean from your sins.

Remember to stay at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting for seven days and seven nights. Do everything that the Lord has commanded, so that he does not punish you with death. This is what the Lord has commanded me.’
So Aaron and his sons did everything that the Lord had told Moses they must do.
9: On the eighth day, Moses told Aaron and his sons and the leaders of Israel to come.
He said to Aaron, ‘Bring a young bull for a sin offering. And bring a male sheep for a burnt offering. They must be perfect animals. Bring them as an offering to the Lord.
Then tell Israel's people, “Bring a male goat for a sin offering. Bring a young cow and a lamb for a burnt offering. The cow and the lamb must be one year old and they must be perfect.

Also bring a bull and a male sheep for a friendship offering that you will offer as a sacrifice to the Lord. And bring a grain offering of flour that is mixed with olive oil. Do this because the Lord will appear to you today.” ’
The people brought all the animals and the grain to the front of the Tent of Meeting as Moses had commanded. All the Israelite people stood together there to worship the Lord.
Moses said to the people, ‘The Lord has commanded you to do this so that his bright glory will appear to you.’

Moses said to Aaron, ‘Go to the altar to offer your sin offering and your burnt offering to the Lord. Do that to make yourself clean from your sins and to make the people clean from their sins. Also offer the people's offerings to make them clean from their sins. The Lord has commanded you to do this.’
So Aaron went to the altar. He killed the young bull that was a sin offering for himself.
Aaron's sons brought the blood to him. He put his finger in the blood and he put it on the four horns of the altar. He poured the blood that remained onto the ground in front of the altar.
Aaron took the bull's fat, its kidneys and the bottom part of its liver. He burnt these parts on the altar, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
But Moses took the meat and the skin of the bull outside the camp. There he completely burned it.
Then Aaron killed the male sheep for the burnt offering. His sons brought the blood to him. He splashed it onto the sides of the altar.
His sons brought the pieces of the sheep to him, including its head. Aaron burned them on the altar.
He washed the sheep's inside parts and its legs. He burned them on top of the burnt offering on the altar.
Then Aaron brought to the altar the male goat which was the sin offering for the people. He killed it as a sacrifice to make the people clean, like the first offering.
Then Aaron brought the animal for the burnt offering. He offered it as a sacrifice in the proper way.
He also brought the grain offering. He took some of it in his hand. He burned it on the altar together with the burnt offering of that morning.
Aaron killed the bull and the male sheep which were friendship offerings for the people. His sons brought the blood to him. He splashed it onto the sides of the altar.
His sons also took the fat pieces from the bull and from the male sheep. They took the fat part of the tail, the fat around the inside parts, the kidneys and the bottom part of the liver.
They put those pieces on the meat of the animal's ribs. Aaron burned the fat pieces of the animals on the altar as an offering.
Then Aaron lifted up the meat of the ribs and the top part of the right leg of each animal as a special offering to the Lord. He did this as Moses had commanded him to do.

Aaron had now finished making the sin offering, the burnt offering and the friendship offering. He lifted up his hands towards the people and he asked God to bless them. Then he came down from the altar.
Moses and Aaron went into the Tent of Meeting. When they came out, they asked God to bless the people. The bright glory of the Lord appeared to all the people.

Then the Lord sent a fire to completely burn the burnt offering and the fat pieces that were on the altar. When the people saw this, they shouted loudly. They fell down with their faces towards the ground.
10: Aaron's sons, Nadab and Abihu, took their pots which held hot coals. They put fire in the pots and they put incense on top. They used it as an offering to the Lord which was wrong. They did it in a way that the Lord had not commanded them to do.
The Lord suddenly sent fire to completely burn Nadab and Abihu. They died there in front of the Lord 's altar.

Then Moses said to Aaron, ‘This is what the Lord said: “People who serve me as priests must respect me. They must realize that I am holy. All the people must know that they should give me honour.” ’ Aaron did not say anything.

Then Moses told Mishael and Elzaphan to come to him. They were sons of Aaron's uncle, Uzziel. Moses said to them, ‘Come here and carry Nadab and Abihu away from the front of the holy tent. Take their bodies to a place outside the camp.’
Nadab and Abihu were still wearing their special robes. Mishael and Elzaphan carried them outside the camp, as Moses had told them to do.

Then Moses spoke to Aaron and to his other sons, Eleazar and Ithamar. He told them, ‘Make your hair look nice. Do not tear your clothes. If you show that you are sad, you will die. The Lord will also be angry with all the people. But your relatives, the Israelite people, should weep. They should be sad because the Lord has killed Nadab and Abihu with fire.
Do not go away from the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. If you do that, you will die, because the Lord has anointed you as his priests.’ So Aaron and his sons did as Moses had said.
Then the Lord said to Aaron,
‘Do not drink any wine before you go into the Tent of Meeting. Do not drink anything that contains alcohol. If you do, you will die. This rule will continue for ever, for all your descendants.
You must know the difference between things that are holy and things that are ordinary. You must know what is clean and what is unclean.
You must teach the Israelites all the laws that I told Moses to give to them.’

Moses said to Aaron and to his sons who still lived, Eleazar and Ithamar, ‘Take the part of the grain offering that you did not burn as a gift to the Lord. Use it to make bread that has no yeast in it. Eat it beside the altar, because it is a very holy offering.

You must eat it in a holy place. It is the part of the grain offering that belongs to you and to your sons. The Lord has given it to you from his special gifts. These are the commands that the Lord has given to me.

The Lord has also given you parts of the offerings that are lifted up to him. He has given you the meat around the ribs and the top part of the back leg. You may eat these parts. Your sons and your daughters may eat them too. You may only eat these parts in a place that is clean. They belong to you and to your sons. The Lord has given them to you from the friendship offerings that the Israelites give to him.

Bring the top of the leg and the meat of the ribs, as well as the fat parts of the animal. Lift them up as a special gift to the Lord. These parts will belong to you and to your descendants. This is a rule that will continue for ever, as the Lord has commanded.’

After that, Moses looked carefully for the male goat that was the sacrifice for the sin offering. He discovered that they had already burnt it on the fire. So Moses was angry with Aaron's sons, Eleazar and Ithamar.

Moses asked them, ‘Why did you not stay near the holy place to eat the meat of the sin offering? It is a very holy offering. The Lord gave you this sacrifice so that the people would not be guilty. It is a sacrifice to make the people clean from their sins.
Look! You did not bring the blood of the goat for the sacrifice into the holy place. So you should have eaten its meat near the holy tent, as I commanded you.’

Aaron said to Moses, ‘Today the people brought their sin offering and their burnt offering to the Lord. But these terrible things have happened to me! If I had eaten the meat of the sin offering today, that would not have pleased the Lord.’
When Moses heard Aaron's reply, he was not angry any more.
11: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron,
‘Tell the Israelites, “These are the animals that you may eat from among the animals which live on the land.
You may eat animals which have feet in two separate parts. They must also be animals which bring their food back into their mouths and eat it again.

Some animals have feet in two separate parts but they do not eat their food twice. Other animals eat their food twice but their feet do not have two separate parts. You must not eat those animals. Camels eat their food twice but their feet are not in two separate parts. So camels are not clean for you to eat.
Rock badgers also eat their food twice but their feet are not in two separate parts. So they are also unclean.
Rabbits also eat their food twice but their feet are not in two separate parts. They are unclean too.
Pigs have feet that are in two completely separate parts. But pigs do not bring their food back to eat it a second time. So pigs are not clean for you to eat.
You must not eat the meat from these animals. You must not touch their dead bodies. They are unclean animals for you.
These are the rules about animals that live in rivers or in the sea. If they have both fins and scales on their bodies, you may eat them.
But you must not eat anything that does not have both fins and scales. That includes fish or any other animals that live in water. They are disgusting for you.
So do not eat their meat or touch their dead bodies.
Any fish or other animals that live in water and do not have both fins and scales are unclean. Keep away from them.
These are the rules about birds. These birds are disgusting so do not eat them: eagles, vultures,
buzzards, kites, all kinds of crows, different kinds of owls, seagulls, hawks, cormorants, ospreys, herons, hoopoes and bats.
These are the rules about insects that fly in the air and also walk on the ground. They are disgusting and you must not eat them.
But some of these insects have legs that they can use to jump. You may eat those kinds of insects.
You may eat any kinds of locusts, crickets and grasshoppers.
But you must not eat any other insects that fly and they also walk on the ground.
All these animals will make you unclean if you touch the dead body of one of them. If you do touch one, you will be unclean until the evening.
If you carry a body of one of these animals, you must wash your clothes. But you will still be unclean until the evening.

Do not touch an animal that does not have feet in two completely separate parts. Do not touch an animal that does not bring food back into its mouth to eat it again. If you touch the dead body of any of these animals, you become unclean.
Do not touch the dead body of an animal that has paws to walk on. If you do touch one, you will be unclean until the evening.
If you carry a body of one of these animals, you must wash your clothes. But you will still be unclean until the evening. These animals are unclean for you.
These are the rules about animals that move near to the ground. These animals are unclean for you: moles, rats, different kinds of lizards, geckos and chameleons.
If you touch the dead body of one of them, you will be unclean until the evening.

If one of them dies and it falls onto something, that thing becomes unclean too. That includes things made of wood, leather or sackcloth, whatever people use them for. You must put it into water. It will be unclean until the evening. Then it will be clean again.
If one of these dead animals falls into a clay pot, everything in the pot becomes unclean. You must break the pot.
If you pour water from the pot onto food, the food becomes unclean. Anything in the pot which you could drink also becomes unclean.

If one of these dead animals falls onto something, that thing becomes unclean, whatever it is. If it is an oven or a cooking pot, you must break it into pieces. It has become unclean and you must not use it.

But if one of these dead animals falls into a spring of water, the water will still be clean. The same is true if it falls into a well where people keep clean water. But the person who removes the dead animal will become unclean.
If one of these dead animals falls onto some dry seeds, they will still be clean. You may plant them in your fields.
But if the seeds are wet and the dead animal falls onto them, they become unclean. Do not use them.
An animal that is good for food might die. If anyone touches its dead body, he will be unclean until the evening.

If anyone eats meat from it, he must wash his clothes. He will still be unclean until the evening. If a person carries the body of the dead animal, he must wash his clothes. He will be unclean until the evening.
You must not eat small animals that move across the ground.
This includes animals that move along the ground on their stomachs and animals that walk on four legs or on many legs. All those things that move near to the ground are disgusting. Do not eat them.
Do not allow those small animals to make you unclean. Do not become disgusting yourselves, as they are.

I am the Lord your God. So keep yourselves separate from these unclean things. You must be holy, because I am holy. Make yourselves holy. Do not allow any of these small animals that move across the ground to make you unclean.
I am the Lord. I brought you out of the land of Egypt so that I would be your God. So you must be holy, because I am holy.
These are the rules about animals which live on the land, about the birds, about all the animals that live in water and about all the animals which move across the ground.
You must learn the difference between animals that are unclean and animals that are clean. You must know which animals you may eat and which animals you must not eat.” ’
12: ‘Tell the Israelites, “When a woman gives birth to a boy, she will be unclean for seven days. She is unclean, as she is when she is bleeding each month.
Eight days after the boy is born, they must circumcise him.

His mother must wait for 33 days after the birth before she becomes clean again. During this time, she must not touch anything that is holy. She must not go into the holy place. After this time, she will become clean.

If a woman gives birth to a girl, the mother will be unclean for two weeks. She is unclean, as she is when she is bleeding each month. She must wait for 66 days after the birth until she becomes clean again.

The mother must wait for the right number of days after she gives birth to a son or a daughter. When those days have finished, she must come to the priest at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. She must bring a lamb that is one year old for a burnt offering. She must also bring a pigeon or a dove for a sin offering.
The priest will offer the gifts to the Lord to make the woman clean again. These are the rules for a mother who gives birth to a boy or a girl.

If the mother does not have enough money to buy a lamb, she may bring two doves or two pigeons instead. One bird will be for a burnt offering. The other bird will be for a sin offering. The priest will offer these to the Lord to make the woman clean again.” ’
13: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron,

‘A person may have a mark on his skin like a sore or a boil. It might become a bad disease that he could give to other people. If he has a mark like that, you must bring him to Aaron or one of his sons, the priests.

The priest must look at the mark on the person's skin. The hair on the mark may have become white, and the mark may go deep under the skin. If that is so, the person has a bad disease in his skin. If the priest sees that, he must say that the person is unclean.

But the mark may be a bright place on the person's skin. It may not go deep under the skin. The hair on the mark may not be white. If those things are true, the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven days.

On the seventh day, the priest must look carefully at the person's skin again. The mark may not have changed. It may not have grown bigger across the skin. If that is true, the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven more days.

On the seventh day, the priest must again look carefully at the person's skin. The mark may not show as clearly as before and it may not have grown any bigger. If that is true, the priest will say that the person is now clean from the disease. The mark on his skin is not dangerous. The person must wash his clothes and then he will be clean.
But after the person has shown his mark to the priest, it might grow bigger across his skin. If that happens, the person must go and show his skin to the priest a second time.
The priest must look carefully at the mark. If it has become bigger, the priest must say that the person is unclean. It is a bad disease of the skin.
If a person has a bad disease in his skin, you must bring him to the priest.
The priest must look carefully at the mark on the person's skin. The mark may be white and it may grow up above the skin. The hair on it may have become white. It may be a sore that has pus in it.

If those things are true, it is a disease that continues for a long time. The priest must say that the person is unclean. The priest does not need to keep him separate from other people. They already know that he is unclean.
It is different if the disease has grown completely across all the person's skin. The priest may see that it covers the whole body, from the person's head to his feet.
The priest must carefully look at the person's skin. If the skin has become white across the person's whole body, the priest must say that the person is clean.
But if there is a sore with pus anywhere on the person's skin, the person is unclean.
The priest must look carefully at the sore and say that the person is unclean. He still has a disease in his skin.
If the sore changes and it becomes white, the person must go to the priest.
The priest must look carefully at the skin. If the skin has become white, the priest will say that the person is now clean again. The disease has finished.
A person might have a boil on his skin. Then it might get better.
There may be a white mark that grows up above the skin where the boil had been. Or there may be a bright mark on the skin that seems to be red. If this happens, the person must go to the priest.

The priest will look carefully at the mark on the person's skin. The mark may seem to grow deep under the skin and the hair on it may be white. If this is true, the priest must say that the person is unclean. The person has a bad disease in the place where the boil had been.

But the priest may see that the mark does not have white hair. It may not go deep under the skin and it may not show very clearly. If that is true, the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven days.
If the mark continues to grow across the skin, the priest must say that the person is unclean. He has a bad disease.
But the mark may not grow bigger across the person's skin. This shows that it is a mark from the boil and it is not a bad disease. The priest must say that the person is clean.
A person may have burnt his skin. Because of that, there might be a mark on his skin that seems to be red or bright white.

The priest must look carefully at the mark. The hair on the bright mark may have become white. The mark may seem to grow deep under the skin. This shows that the place where the person burnt himself has become a bad disease in his skin. The priest must say that the person is unclean. He might give the disease to other people.

But the priest may see that the mark does not have white hair. It may not go deep under the skin and it may not show very clearly. If that is true, the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven days.
On the seventh day, the priest must look carefully at the mark. If the mark is growing bigger across the skin, the priest must say that the person is unclean. He has a bad disease.

But the mark may not have grown across the person's skin. It may not show so clearly. This shows that it is a mark from the place where the person burnt his skin. The priest must say that the person is clean.
A man or a woman may have a sore on their head or on their chin.

The priest must look carefully at the sore. The sore may seem to go deep under the skin. It may have hair in it that is thin and yellow. If this is true, the priest must say that the person is unclean. This kind of sore is a bad disease on the person's head or on their chin.
But the sore may not go deep below the skin. There may be no hair in it that is black. If the priest sees that, he must keep the person separate from other people for seven days.
On the seventh day, the priest must look carefully at the sore. The sore may not have grown bigger. There may be no hair in it that is yellow. It may not go deep under the skin.

If that is true, the person must cut the hair off their head. But they must not cut the hair off where the sore is. Then the priest must keep the person separate from other people for seven more days.

On the seventh day, the priest must look carefully at the sore again. The sore may not have grown bigger and it may not go deep under the person's skin. Then the priest must say that the person is clean. The person must wash their clothes and then they will be clean.
But after that, the sore may grow bigger again across the person's skin.
If that happens, the priest must look carefully at the sore. If it has become bigger, the person is unclean. The priest does not need to look for yellow hair.
But the sore may not have changed. Black hair may have started to grow in it. If that is true, the disease has left the person. The priest must say that the person is clean.
A man or a woman may have white marks on their skin.
The priest must look carefully at the marks. If the colour of the marks is not bright, it is not a dangerous disease in the skin. The person is clean.
The hair may disappear from a man's head until he is bald. That man is clean.
If the hair disappears from the front of the man's head until that part is bald, the man is clean.
But if there is a red sore on the bald place at the top or at the back of the man's head, it is a bad disease.

The priest must look carefully at the sore. The sore in the bald place may grow up above the skin and it may seem to be red. If it looks like a skin disease that appears in other places of a person's body,
the man has a bad disease. He is unclean. The priest must say that the man is unclean because of the sore on his head.
Any person who has a bad disease in his skin must tear his clothes. He must not make his hair look nice. He must cover the lower part of his face. He must shout, “I am unclean.”
All the time that he has the bad skin disease he will continue to be unclean. He must live in a separate place from other people, outside the camp.
Mould may grow on clothes that are made of wool or linen.
It may grow on any piece of linen or wool, or on anything made of leather.
If the mould on any of these things seems to be green or red, it is a bad mould. The person who has the piece of cloth must show it to the priest.
The priest must look carefully at the mould. He must keep the thing that has the mould on it separate from other things for seven days.

On the seventh day, the priest must look at the mould again. If the place that has the mould is growing bigger, it is a dangerous mould. Whatever the thing is made of, cloth or leather, it is unclean.
The priest must burn the piece of cloth or the piece of leather which has the mould. Because it has a dangerous mould, the priest must use fire to destroy it.
When the priest looks at the piece of cloth or leather on the seventh day, the mould might not have grown bigger.
If that is true, he must tell the person to wash the thing that has the mould. Then he must keep it separate from other things for seven more days.

The priest must again look carefully at the piece of cloth after the person has washed it. If the mould still looks the same, the piece of cloth is unclean. Even if the mould has not grown bigger, the cloth is unclean. You must use fire to destroy the piece of cloth or leather. You must do this whether the mould is on the inside or the outside of it.

When the priest looks at the piece of cloth after the person has washed it, the mould may not show so clearly. If that happens, the priest must tear the part that has mould on it out of the piece of cloth or leather.

After that, if the mould appears again in the piece of cloth or the piece of leather, it is a dangerous mould that may destroy other things. You must use fire to destroy the thing that has the mould on it.
But if the mould disappears after you have washed it the first time, it is no longer dangerous. You must wash the piece of cloth or leather a second time. Then it will be clean.’

These are the rules for mould that grows on cloth that is made of wool or linen. They are also the rules for mould that grows on leather things. They tell the priest how to decide if the things are clean or unclean.
14: The Lord said to Moses,
‘These rules are for a person who has had a bad disease in his skin. When the disease has finished, you must bring him to the priest to make him clean.
The priest must go outside the camp and he must look carefully at the person's skin. If the disease has gone away,

this is what the priest must do. He must tell someone to bring two birds that are alive. They must be birds that are good for food. They must also bring a piece of wood from a cedar tree, some red wool and a branch of hyssop. The priest will use these to make the person clean.
The priest will command someone to kill one of the birds. He must do that over a clay pot that has fresh water in it. The blood of the dead bird will mix with the water.
The priest will take the living bird, the piece of cedar wood, the red wool and the hyssop. He will make them wet with the blood of the dead bird.

Then the priest will shake the blood over the person who had the disease. He will do this seven times. The priest will then say that the person is clean. He will let the living bird go so that it flies away.

The person who has become well from the disease must wash his clothes. He must cut off all his hair and wash his body with water. Then he will be clean. After that, he can go into the camp. But he must stay outside his tent for seven days.
On the seventh day, he must completely cut off all his hair. That includes all the hair of his head and his face. He must wash his clothes. He must wash his body with water. Then he will be clean.

On the eighth day, the person must bring two male lambs and one female lamb that is one year old. Both the animals must be perfect. He must also bring three tenths of an ephah of the best flour that is mixed with olive oil as a grain offering. And he must bring a cup of olive oil.
He must take these to the priest who is making him clean. The priest must tell the person to stand at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must bring his offerings to the Lord there.
The priest must take one of the male lambs and the cup of olive oil. He must lift them up to the Lord as a special gift.

He must kill the male lamb in the holy place where they kill the animals for sin offerings and burnt offerings. The guilt offering belongs to the priest, as the sin offering does. It is a very holy offering.

The priest must put some of the blood from the guilt offering on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He must put some blood on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he must put some blood on the big toe of the person's right foot.
The priest must pour some of the olive oil from the cup into his own left hand.
He must put one finger of his right hand into the oil that is in his left hand. He must shake the oil seven times in front of the Lord.

The priest must then take the oil that remains in his left hand. He must put some of it on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He must put some of the oil on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he must put some of it on the big toe of the person's right foot. He will put the oil on top of the blood from the offering.
The priest must take the oil that still remains in his left hand. He must put it on the person's head. In this way the priest will make the person clean from his disease in front of the Lord.
Then the priest must kill the animal for the sin offering. This makes the person who had the disease clean from his sins. After that, the priest will kill the animal for the burnt offering.
He will offer this as a sacrifice on the altar, together with the grain offering. The priest will do all these things to make the person who had the disease completely clean.

If the person is too poor to bring all these offerings, he may bring these things instead. He must bring one male lamb as a guilt offering to lift up in front of the Lord. That will be an offering to make him clean from his disease. He must also bring a tenth of an ephah of the best flour that is mixed with olive oil as a grain offering. And he must bring a cup of olive oil.
The person must also bring two birds that he has enough money to buy. They must be doves or pigeons. One bird will be for a sin offering and the other one will be for a burnt offering.
On the eighth day, the person must bring his offerings to the Lord. He must bring them to the priest at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting to make himself clean.
The priest will take the male lamb and the cup of oil. He will lift them up as a special gift to the Lord.

Then he will kill the lamb which is the guilt offering. He will put some of its blood on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He will put some of the blood on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he will put some of it on the big toe of the person's right foot.
The priest will pour some of the oil into his own left hand.
He will put one finger of his right hand into the oil. He will shake the oil seven times in front of the Lord.

He will put some oil from his hand on the lowest part of the person's right ear. He will also put some oil on the thumb of the person's right hand. And he will put some oil on the big toe of the person's right foot. He will put the oil on the same places that he put the blood from the guilt offering.
The priest must take the oil that still remains in his left hand. He must put it on the person's head. In that way the priest will make the person clean from his disease in front of the Lord.
Then the priest will offer the pigeons or the doves that the person has been able to buy.

One bird will be for a sin offering. The other will be for a burnt offering. The priest will offer these together with the grain offering. In this way the priest will make the person clean in front of the Lord.
These are the rules for a poor person who had a bad disease in his skin. These are the offerings he must bring to make himself clean if he is too poor to bring all the usual offerings.’
The Lord said to Moses and Aaron,
‘One day you will go into Canaan, the land that I am giving to you as your home. When you are living there, I may cause mould to come in one of your houses.
If a person finds mould in his house, he must tell the priest. He must say, “I can see something like a bad mould in my house.”

The priest will tell the person to take everything out of the house. If the person does not do that, all the things in the house will become unclean. He must do it before the priest goes to look at the house.
The priest will look carefully at the walls of the house. He will see if the mould has green or red marks in it. He will see if the mould goes deep into the wall.
If the priest sees those things, he will go outside the house. He will lock it to stay shut for seven days.
On the seventh day, the priest will return to the house. He will look carefully at the mould again. It may have grown bigger across the walls of the house.
If that has happened, the priest must say, “You must remove the stones that have mould on them. You must take them outside the town. You must put them in a special place for unclean things.”
The person must then remove the clay that covers all the inside walls of the house. He must put the clay outside the town in a place for unclean things.
The person who lives in the house must get some other stones and some more clay. He will use those instead of the unclean stones and clay that he removed.
After he has done all those things, the person might see that the mould has come back again.
The priest will come to the house and look carefully at the mould. If the mould has grown again across the walls, this shows that it is a dangerous mould. The house is unclean.
The person must completely knock down his house. He must remove all the stones, the pieces of wood and the clay. He must put them all outside the town in a place for unclean things.
A person might go into the house during the time that the priest has shut it because of the mould. That person will become unclean until evening.
Anyone who sleeps in the house must wash their clothes. And anyone who eats in the house must also wash their clothes.
After the person has mended his house, the priest may see that the mould has not grown again. The priest will then say that the house is clean.
He must take two birds, a piece of wood from a cedar tree, some red wool and a branch of hyssop. He will use these to make the house clean.
He will kill one of the birds over a clay pot that has fresh water in it. The blood of the dead bird will mix with the water.

The priest will then take the piece of cedar wood, the hyssop, the red wool and the other bird that is still alive. He will make them wet with the blood of the dead bird and the fresh water. He will shake the blood and water over the house seven times.
In this way, the priest will make the house clean. He will use the blood of the dead bird, the fresh water, the living bird, the piece of cedar wood, the hyssop and the red wool.
Then the priest will take the living bird outside the town. He will let the bird fly away over the fields. In this way he will make the house clean from the mould.’
These are the rules for all kinds of bad disease.
They include rules for mould that grows on cloth or on the walls of a house.
They include rules for sores and boils and marks on someone's skin.
They show if a person has a dangerous disease in their skin. They show whether the person is clean or he is unclean.
15: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron,
‘Tell Israel's people, “If any kind of pus is coming out of a man's body, it makes the man unclean.
It does not matter whether the pus continues to leave the man's body or it stays inside. It causes the man to be unclean.
If the man lies on a bed, it becomes unclean. Anything that the man sits on also becomes unclean.
Anyone who touches the bed must wash his clothes and his whole body. He will continue to be unclean until evening.
If anyone sits on anything that the man has sat on, they will also become unclean. So they must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
Someone might touch the man who has pus coming from his body. If that happens, they must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
The man who is unclean might spit on another person. Then the other person must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
The man might sit on something to ride on a horse or a donkey. Whatever he sits on will become unclean.
Someone might touch or carry any of the things that the man has sat on. Then that person must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.

The unclean man might not wash his hands before he touches another person. The person that he touched then becomes unclean. That person must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
If the unclean man touches a clay pot, you must break it. If he touches a wooden bowl, you must wash it with clean water.

The man who has had the pus coming from his body might become well again. He must count seven days after the pus has gone. Then he must wash his clothes. He must wash his whole body in clean water. Then he will be clean.
On the eighth day, he must bring two birds to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. They must be doves or pigeons. He must give them to the priest to offer to the Lord.

The priest will use one of the birds for a sin offering. He will use the other bird for a burnt offering. In this way the priest will make the man clean before the Lord. The pus that came from his body no longer causes him to be unclean.
When semen comes out of a man by mistake, he must wash his whole body. He will be unclean until evening.
He must wash any clothes or leather things that has his semen on them. They will be unclean until evening.
When a man has sex with a woman, some of his semen might splash out. If that happens, they must both wash their bodies. They will be unclean until evening.
Blood comes out from a woman's body every month. When this happens, she will be unclean for seven days. If anyone touches her during this time, they will be unclean until evening.
During the time that the woman is bleeding, if she lies on anything, it becomes unclean. And if she sits on anything, it becomes unclean.
If anyone touches her bed, they must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
If anyone touches something that the woman has sat on, they too must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
This is true for anything that the woman has sat on, including her bed. If anyone touches any of these things, they will be unclean until evening.
A man might have sex with a woman during the time that she is bleeding. If her blood touches him, he will be unclean for seven days. Any bed that the man lies on also becomes unclean.

A woman is unclean all the time that blood is coming out of her body. This is true if it happens at a different time in the month. It is also true if it continues at the usual time for more than seven days. It is all the same. She is unclean.
The rule is the same as when she is bleeding at the usual time of the month. During any time that she is bleeding, anything that she sits on or she lies on becomes unclean.
Anyone who touches those things will also be unclean. They must wash their clothes and their whole body. They will be unclean until evening.
When the woman stops bleeding, she must count seven days. Then she will be clean.
On the eighth day, she must bring two birds to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. They must be doves or pigeons. She must give them to the priest.
The priest will use one of the birds for a sin offering. He will use the other bird for a burnt offering. In this way the priest will make the woman who had been bleeding clean before the Lord.

In this way you will keep Israel's people separate from things that make them unclean. They will not cause my tabernacle to become unclean. That is the place where I live among them. If they make that place unclean, I would punish them with death.” ’
These are the rules for anyone who has any kind of pus that comes out of their body. Or it may be semen that comes from a man's sex part. These things cause him to be unclean.

They are also the rules for a woman during the time when she bleeds each month. They are the rules for any man or woman who becomes unclean because of these things. They are also the rules for a man who has sex with a woman during the time that she is unclean.
16: The Lord spoke to Moses after two of Aaron's sons had died. They had burned incense in front of the Lord in a way that was wrong. The Lord punished them with death.

The Lord said to Moses, ‘Tell your brother Aaron that he must not go behind the curtain into the Most Holy Place of the tabernacle. He must not do that at any time that he wants to. I appear in a cloud over the lid of the Covenant Box that is in there. So Aaron will die if he goes in there when it is not the proper time.
This is what Aaron must do when he goes into the holy place. He must bring a young bull for a sin offering. He must also bring a male sheep for a burnt offering.

He must wash his whole body before he puts on his special clothes, because they are holy. He must wear a holy linen robe and linen trousers next to his skin. He must tie a linen belt around his body. He must put a linen hat on his head.
Israel's people must also give him two male goats for a sin offering and one male sheep for a burnt offering.
Aaron must offer the bull as a sin offering. This will make him clean from his own sins. It will also make the people in his house clean from their sins.
Then he must take the two goats to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. There he must offer them to the Lord.
He must throw special stones to decide what he should do with each goat. One stone will have a mark for the Lord. The other stone will have a mark for Azazel.
Aaron must take the goat that the stone showed is for the Lord. He must kill it as a sin offering.

But he must bring the other goat in front of the Lord while it is alive. He will send it away to escape into the desert. In that way, it will take away the sins of the people and it will make them clean.
Aaron must offer the bull which is for a sin offering. This will make him clean from his own sins. It will also make the people in his house clean from their sins.

He must take a pot of hot coals that burn on the altar in front of the Lord. He must fill both his hands with incense that has a sweet smell. He must take the hot coals and the incense with him behind the curtain.
Then he must put the incense onto the hot coals in front of the Lord. The smoke from the incense will rise up over the lid of the Covenant Box. If he does this, he will not die.

Then he must take the blood from the bull. He must use his finger to splash blood onto the lid of the Covenant Box. He must also splash some blood seven times with his finger in front of the Covenant Box.

Aaron must then kill the goat which is the sin offering for the people. He must take the goat's blood behind the curtain. He must use it in the same way that he used the bull's blood. He must shake it on the lid of the Covenant Box and in front of the Covenant Box.

In this way Aaron will make the Most Holy Place clean. He must do this because of the sins of Israel's people that make them unclean. He must do the same thing for the whole Tent of Meeting to make it clean. The tent stands among Israel's people who are unclean.

While Aaron is doing this, nobody may go into the Tent of Meeting. They must wait while he goes into the Most Holy Place to make everything clean. He must also make himself, his family and all the people of Israel clean from their sins. Everyone must wait outside until Aaron comes out of the Tent of Meeting.

When he comes out, he must go to the altar that stands in front of the Lord 's tent. He must make the altar clean. He must take some of the bull's blood and some of the goat's blood. He must put some blood on all the four horns of the altar.
Then he must splash some of the blood on the altar seven times with his finger. In this way he will make it clean for the Lord to use. He must do this because Israel's people are unclean.
Aaron must then bring the goat that is alive in front of the Lord. He must do this after he has made clean the Most Holy Place, the Tent of Meeting and the altar.

He must put his hands on the goat's head. He must speak aloud all the sins that Israel's people have done. He must say that they have been wicked and they have not obeyed the Lord. In this way he will put all their sins on the goat's head. Aaron must then tell a man that he has chosen to send the goat away into the desert.
The goat will carry all the sins of Israel's people far away to an empty place. The man must send it away into the desert.
After this, Aaron must go into the Tent of Meeting. He must take off his special linen clothes. He had put them on when he went into the holy tent. He must leave the clothes there.

He must wash his whole body in a holy place. He must put on his own clothes and go out of the tent. Then he will offer the animals for his own burnt offering and the burnt offering for the people. In this way, he will make himself clean and the people clean from their sins.
Then he must burn on the altar the fat from the sin offering.
The man who sent the goat away to escape must wash his clothes and his whole body. Then he may return to the camp.

Someone must carry the dead bodies of the animals for the sin offerings outside the camp. That is the bull and the goat that Aaron had killed. He used their blood to make the Most Holy Place clean. The man who takes them outside the camp must completely burn them. That includes their skin, their meat and their inside parts.
After that, the man must wash his clothes and his whole body. Then he may return to the camp.

On the tenth day of the seventh month, you must not eat and you must not do any work. This is a rule that you must obey every year for all time. It is a rule for you who belong to Israel's people and for foreign people who live among you.
It will be the Day of Atonement. On this day the priest will make you clean. The Lord will forgive you for all your sins.
