It is a very holy day of complete rest for you. You must not eat during that day. This is a rule that you must all obey every year.
The descendant of Aaron who is chosen as leader of the priests will do all these things on the Day of Atonement. He will put on the linen clothes that are holy.
He will make clean the Most Holy Place, the Tent of Meeting and the altar. He will make the priests and all the people clean from their sins.
Once every year, on this day, he will make Israel's people clean from all their sins. This is a rule that you must obey for all time.’ Moses did all these things as the Lord had commanded him.
17: ‘Speak to Aaron, to his sons and to all Israel's people. Tell them, “This is what the Lord has commanded.

If any Israelite wants to kill a bull, a lamb or a goat as a sacrifice, he must bring it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must bring it there as an offering to the Lord. If he kills the animal anywhere else in the camp or outside the camp, he is guilty of a sin. He has poured out the animal's blood in a wrong way. He can no longer belong among Israel's people.

This rule will stop the people killing animals as sacrifices to the Lord in the fields. Instead, they must bring their offerings to the priest at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. They must kill the animals there as friendship offerings to the Lord.
The priest must splash the animal's blood on the Lord 's altar at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must also burn the animal's fat. It will make a smell that gives pleasure to the Lord.
The people must be faithful to the Lord. They must no longer worship the idols of goats and offer sacrifices to them. The people and their descendants must always obey this rule.”
You must also tell the people, “An Israelite or a foreigner may want to kill an animal as a burnt offering or as another sacrifice.
If so, he must bring it to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. He must offer it there to the Lord. If he does not do that, he can no longer belong among Israel's people.
Any Israelite or a foreigner who lives among you must not eat any blood. If anyone does that, I will turn against that person. I will remove him so that he no longer belongs among Israel's people.
Everything that lives has life because of its blood. I have told you to offer blood on the altar. That is how you become clean from your sins. The animal's blood is the life that makes you clean.
For this reason I have told Israel's people that you must not eat blood. And no foreign person who lives among you may eat blood either.

Israelites or foreigners who live among them may catch a wild animal or a bird. If it is clean to eat, the person who catches it must pour its blood on the ground. He must cover the blood with some soil.
All living things have life because of their blood. So I have told the Israelites that they must not eat the blood of any living thing. Anyone who eats blood can no longer belong among my people.

An Israelite or a foreigner may find the body of an animal that has died by itself. Or it may be the body of an animal that wild animals have killed. If he eats the meat of that animal, he must wash his clothes and his whole body. He will be unclean until evening. Then he will become clean.
But if he does not wash his clothes and his whole body, he will receive the punishment that he deserves.” ’
18: The Lord said to Moses,
‘Speak to Israel's people. Say to them, “I am the Lord your God.

You have been living in Egypt, but you must not do the things that the people of Egypt do. I will soon take you to live in the land of Canaan. You must not do the things that the people of Canaan do. You must not live in the way that they think is right.
Instead, you must live in the way that I say is right. You must obey my laws. I am the Lord your God.
You must obey my laws and my teaching. Anyone who does that will continue to live. I am the Lord.”
Tell them this: “You must not have sex with anyone who belongs to your own family. I am the Lord.
Do not have sex with your mother. That would cause your father to be ashamed.
Do not have sex with any wife of your father. Your father would be ashamed.

Do not have sex with your sister. She may be your mother's daughter or your father's daughter. She may have been born in your house or in another house. It does not matter. You must not have sex with any sister.
Do not have sex with any granddaughter. That would make you ashamed.
Do not have sex with the daughter of your father and any of his wives. She is your sister so you must not have sex with her.
Do not have sex with your father's sister. She belongs to your father's family.
Do not have sex with your mother's sister. She belongs to your mother's family.
Do not have sex with the wife of your father's brother. That would make your uncle ashamed. His wife is your aunt. You must not try to get near to her.
Do not have sex with your son's wife.
Do not have sex with your brother's wife. That would make your brother ashamed.

Do not have sex with both a woman and her daughter. And do not take the woman's son's daughter or her daughter's daughter so that you can have sex with any of them. They belong to the same family. It would be a very wicked thing to do.
If your wife is still alive, do not marry her sister as a second wife. While your wife lives, you must not have sex with her sister.
Do not have sex with a woman at the time when she bleeds each month. She is unclean during that time.
Do not have sex with the wife of another man. You would become unclean.
Do not give any of your children as an offering to the god Molech. That would bring shame to the name of your God. I am the Lord!
A man must not have sex with another man as he would have sex with a woman. That is a disgusting thing to do.
Do not have sex with any animal. That would make you unclean. A woman must not offer herself to an animal to have sex with it. She would be doing a very bad thing.

Do not do any of these things that would make you unclean. The nations who live in Canaan make themselves unclean with all these things. I will chase out those people so that you can live in their land.
The land itself has become unclean because the people did these things. So I am punishing those people for their sins. The land has thrown out the people who lived on it.
But you yourselves must obey my laws and my rules. Do not do any of these wicked things. That includes the foreign people who live among you.
The people who lived in Canaan before you did these disgusting things. Because of that, the land became unclean.
So do not make the land unclean, as those people have done. Do not cause the land to throw you out, as it did to the nations who lived there before you.
If anyone does any of these disgusting things, they can no longer belong among Israel's people.
You must obey what I have commanded you to do. Do not live in the disgusting way that these other people have lived. Do not make yourselves unclean like that. I am the Lord your God.” ’
19: The Lord said to Moses,
‘Speak to all Israel's people. Tell them, “You must be holy because I am the Lord your God and I am holy.
Each of you must respect his mother and his father. You must keep the Sabbath day as a day of rest. I am the Lord your God.
Do not turn away from me to worship idols. Do not use metal to make images of gods for yourselves. I am the Lord your God.
When you offer a friendship offering as a sacrifice to me, do it in the right way. Then I will accept your gift.
When you kill an animal for the sacrifice, eat its meat on the same day. You may eat what remains on the second day but you must completely burn any meat that remains on the third day.
If anyone eats the meat on the third day, it will not please me. I will not accept their offering.
The person who has eaten the meat on the third day deserves punishment. He has caused a gift that is holy to the Lord to become unclean. That person can no longer belong to Israel's people.
At harvest time, when you cut the crops on your land, do not cut to the edge of your field. Do not pick up the grain that falls onto the ground.

Do not pick all the grapes off your vines. Do not pick up the grapes that have fallen onto the ground. Leave some of these for the poor people and for the foreign people who live among you. I am the Lord your God.
You must not take things that belong to other people. You must not deceive other people or tell them lies.
Do not use my name to promise something that you will not do. That would bring shame to the name of your God. I am the Lord.
Do not cheat anyone. Do not rob anyone. If you are paying someone to work for you, pay them the same day.
You must not curse a deaf person. Do not put something in front of a blind person, to make them fall down. Respect and obey me as your God. I am the Lord.
When you judge other people, do this in a way that is fair. Do not be more kind to a rich person than to a poor person. You must be fair to everyone.
Do not say bad things about anyone for other people to hear. If somebody is in danger of death, speak the truth to help them if you can. Do not keep quiet. I am the Lord.
You must not think of ways to hurt another person. If someone has done a wrong thing, warn them about it. Then you will not be guilty of a sin yourself.
Do not try to punish another Israelite who has done something wrong against you. Do not try to hurt them. Instead, you must love other people as much as you love yourself. I am the Lord.

You must obey my rules. You must not allow two different kinds of animals to have babies together. Do not put two different kinds of seeds in one field. Do not wear clothes that are made from two different kinds of material.

If a man has chosen a slave girl to marry, no other man may have sex with her. She may not have become a free woman. If so, any other man who has sex with her must pay money to the first man. But you must not punish them with death, because the girl was still a slave.
The man must bring a male sheep to the Lord at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting. That will be his guilt offering.
The priest will offer the sheep to the Lord to make the man clean from his sin. Then the Lord will forgive the man for the sin that he has done.
When you go into Canaan, you will plant trees that have fruit to eat. But the fruit will not be clean for you to eat. You must not eat the fruit for three years.
In the fourth year, all the fruit will be holy. Use it as an offering to praise the Lord.
In the fifth year, you may eat the fruit from your trees. If you do this, you will have more food to eat at harvest time. I am the Lord your God.
You must not eat any meat that still has blood in it. Do not use magic to learn about future times or other things.
Do not cut the hair from the sides of your head or from the edges of your beard.
Do not cut marks in your body to show that you are sad about a dead person. Do not cut pictures into your skin. I am the Lord.
Do not cause your daughter to be a prostitute. That would bring shame on her. It would also cause many people to turn away from me and do wicked things.
You must obey my rules about Sabbath days. Respect the holy place where you meet with me. I am the Lord.
Do not try to speak to the spirits of dead people. Do not ask other people to do this for you. That will make you unclean. I am the Lord your God.
You must stand up to give honour to old people. Respect and obey me, your God. I am the Lord.
Do not be cruel to foreign people who live among you.
Be kind to them, as you are to your own people. Love foreigners as much as you love yourselves. Remember that you yourselves lived in Egypt as foreigners. I am the Lord your God.
Always be fair when you measure things. When you measure how long, how heavy or how big something is,
always use correct weights and measures. Use the correct size of bowl to measure grain or oil. I am the Lord your God. I led you out of the land of Egypt.
You must always obey all my laws and my rules. I am the Lord.” ’
20: The Lord said to Moses,

‘Speak to Israel's people. Say to them, “Nobody among you must ever give any of his children as an offering to the god Molech. That includes you Israelites and the foreign people who live in Israel. If anyone does that, you must punish him with death. Israel's people must throw stones at him to kill him.

I myself will turn against that person so that he no longer belongs among Israel's people. He deserves that punishment because he has offered some of his children to Molech. He has caused my holy place to become unclean. He has brought shame to my holy name.
The people of that man's town may decide not to punish him. He has offered some of his children to Molech, but they might refuse to punish him with death.

If that happens, I myself will turn against that man and his family. I will cause him to become separate from his own people. I will do that to everyone who turns away from me to worship Molech as he has done.

You must not try to speak to the spirits of dead people. Do not ask anyone to do that for you. Anyone who does that is no longer faithful to me. I will turn against that person so that he no longer belongs among Israel's people.
I am the Lord your God, so you must be faithful to me. You must be holy.
You must always be careful to obey my laws. I am the Lord and I cause you to be holy.
If anyone curses his mother or his father, you must punish him with death. He deserves to die for his sin.
If a man has sex with another man's wife you must punish both of them with death.
If a man has sex with his father's wife, he has caused his father to be ashamed. You must punish both of them with death. They deserve to die for their sin.
If a man has sex with his son's wife, you must punish both of them with death. They have done a very bad thing. They deserve to die for their sin.
If a man has sex with another man, you must punish both of them with death. They have done a disgusting thing together. They deserve to die for their sin.
If a man marries a woman and her mother, that is a wicked thing. You must burn the man and both the women in a fire until they die. That will stop wicked things like that from happening among you.
If a man has sex with an animal, you must punish him with death. You must also kill the animal.
If a woman offers herself to an animal to have sex with it, you must punish her with death. You must also kill the animal. They both deserve to die for this sin.

If a man takes his sister to have sex with her, it is a terrible sin. It does not matter whether she is the daughter of his father or the daughter of his mother. You must remove them from among their people. The man has brought shame on his sister. He must receive the punishment that he deserves.
A man must not have sex with a woman during the time when she is bleeding. If he does that, they have had sex while she is unclean They can no longer belong among Israel's people.
A man must not have sex with his mother's sister or with his father's sister. If he does that, he has brought shame on someone from his own family. They must receive the punishment that they deserve.
If a man has sex with his aunt, he brings shame on his uncle. They must receive the punishment that they deserve for their sin. Neither of them will ever have any children.
If a man marries his brother's wife, that makes him unclean. Neither of them will have any children. The man has brought shame on his brother.
You must always obey all my laws and my rules. If you do that, the land where I am taking you to live will not throw you out.
I will chase out the nations who live there now, so that you can live there. You must not live in the way that those people live. I hate them because of all the disgusting things that they have done.

I have told you that the land will belong to you as your home. It is a land where there is plenty of food and drink, enough for everyone. I am the Lord your God. I have chosen you to be separate from other people.

So you must know the difference between animals that are clean and animals that are unclean. You must know the difference between birds that are clean and birds that are unclean. You must not eat anything that I have told you is unclean. That includes any small thing that moves across the ground.
I am the Lord and I am holy. So you must be holy as my people. I have chosen you to be separate from other people to belong to me.

Any man or woman who talks with the spirits of dead people must die. Anyone who uses magic to learn about future times must also die. You must throw stones at that person until they die. They deserve to die for their sin.” ’
21: The Lord said to Moses, ‘Speak to Aaron's sons, the priests. Tell them, “A priest must not touch a dead body. That would make him unclean among his people.
But if it is someone from his family who has died, he may touch their body. It may be his mother, his father, his son, his daughter or his brother.
He may touch the dead body of his sister if she has no husband to take care of her. The priest may make himself unclean for her.
But a priest must not touch the dead body of someone who belongs to his family because of marriage. He must not make himself unclean for that person.
A priest must not cut the hair off his head. He must not cut the edges of his beard. He must not cut marks in his body.
Priests must be holy to serve me. They must not bring shame to the name of their God. They are the people who offer gifts to the Lord. They bring food to their God. So they must be holy people.
A priest must not marry a woman who has been a prostitute. He must not marry a woman who has become separate from her husband. A priest must be holy to serve his God.
You must respect priests as holy people, because they offer food to your God. I am the Lord and I am holy. I am the one who causes you to be holy.
If a priest's daughter becomes a prostitute, she is unclean. She has brought shame on her father. You must burn her in a fire until she dies.

They have poured special oil on the head of the leader of the priests. He is able to wear the special clothes for priests. This shows that he is greater than his brothers. So he must always make his hair look nice. He must not tear his clothes.
He must not go anywhere that is near a dead body. Even if it is his father or his mother who has died, he must not make himself unclean for them.
He must not leave the holy place to go to a funeral. That would cause the holy place to be unclean. The oil that they poured on his head shows that I have chosen him as my servant. I am the Lord.
The leader of the priests must marry a woman who has not had sex before.
He must not marry a widow or a woman who has become separate from her husband. He must not marry a woman who has been a prostitute. Instead, he must choose a Levite woman who has not had sex before.
I, the Lord, have caused priests to be holy. So he must not marry a woman who would cause his children to be unclean.” ’
The Lord said to Moses,
‘Tell Aaron, “Your descendants may only serve me as priests if they have a perfect body. If not, they must not bring offerings of food to their God. This rule will continue for all time.

No man may serve me if he has anything wrong with his body. He must not be blind. He must not have a weak leg or one leg that is shorter than the other leg. He must not have any part of his face that is the wrong shape.
He must not have an arm or a leg that is broken.
He must not have a back that is not straight. He must not be very small and thin. He must not have an eye disease or a skin disease. He must not have testicles that are spoiled.

No descendant of Aaron who has something wrong with his body may serve me as a priest. He may not offer gifts to me, the Lord. His body is not perfect, so he may not bring offerings of food to his God.
He may eat the holy food offerings and the most holy food offerings.

But he must not go near the curtain in the Holy Place. He must not go up to the altar. This is because he has something wrong with his body. He must not make the holy places unclean. I am the Lord. I cause the priests to be holy.” ’
Moses told all these rules to Aaron, to his sons and to all Israel's people.
22: The Lord said to Moses,

‘Tell Aaron and his sons to be careful with the holy offerings from Israel's people. Those are gifts that the people offer to me. The priests must not spoil the honour of my holy name. I am the Lord. Tell them this, as a rule that will continue for ever:

No priest may come near to the holy offerings when he is unclean. Those are gifts that Israel's people have offered to me, the Lord. If any unclean priest goes near them, he can never serve me again. I am the Lord.

If any descendant of Aaron is unclean, he must not eat the holy offerings until he becomes clean again. He may be unclean because of a skin disease, or some kind of pus is coming out from his body. He may have touched something that has touched a dead body and is unclean. He may have touched a man whose semen has come out from his sex part.
He may have touched an unclean animal that moves near to the ground. He may have touched a person who is unclean for any reason. All these things will cause the priest himself to become unclean.
Any priest who touches any of these unclean things will remain unclean until evening. He must not eat any of the holy offerings until he has washed his whole body.
When the sun goes down, he will be clean. Then he can eat the holy offerings which are his food.
A priest may find the body of an animal that has died by itself, or wild animals have killed it. He must not eat the meat of that animal. It will make him unclean. I am the Lord.
Priests must obey my commands. If they do not obey them, they will be guilty of sin. They will die because they have not respected my commands. I am the Lord. I cause the priests to be holy.
Only people who belong to a priest's family may eat any of the holy offerings. No visitor who is living in the priest's house may do that. No servant that the priest pays to work for him may do that.
But a slave that the priest has bought for himself may eat the holy offerings. And a slave that was born in the priest's house may eat them.
If a priest's daughter marries a man who is not a priest, she may not eat the holy offerings.

But if her husband dies or she becomes separate from him, it is different. She may have no children to take care of her. Then she may return to live in her father's home where she lived when she was young. If so, she is allowed to eat some of the food that her father receives as a priest. Only someone who belongs to a priest's family may eat that food.
Someone might eat a holy offering by mistake. If that happens, he must pay the priest for that food. He must also add one fifth of its value.
The priests must always respect the holy offerings as special food. They are gifts which Israel's people offer to the Lord.
If the priests allow other people to eat the holy offerings, those people are guilty of sin. They will receive punishment. I am the Lord and I cause the offerings to be holy.’
The Lord said to Moses,

‘Speak to Aaron, to his sons and to all Israel's people. Tell them, “This is the rule about burnt offerings from the people of Israel or from the foreign people who live among them. It may be an offering that they have promised to give to the Lord. Or it may be an offering that they choose to give.
I will only accept your gift if it is a perfect male animal. It can be a bull, a sheep or a goat.
You must not offer to me any animal that has something wrong with it. If it is not perfect, I will not accept it as a gift from you.

This is also the rule if someone brings an animal as a sacrifice for a friendship offering. He may do that because of a special promise. Or he may do it because he chooses to give it. It may be a bull, a sheep or a goat. I will only accept it if the animal is perfect. It must not have anything wrong with it.

You must not offer to the Lord an animal that is blind. It must not have a broken bone or a body that has become spoiled. It must not have sores or any other disease on its skin. You must not offer on the altar any animal like that as a gift to the Lord.

You may bring a bull or a sheep that has a leg which is longer or shorter than the other legs. I will accept that animal as a gift that you choose to bring to me. I will not accept that animal as an offering that you make because of a promise.

You must not offer to the Lord an animal that has something wrong with its testicles. I will not accept the animal if anyone has hurt its testicles or removed them. You must never hurt the testicles of any animal in your land.

You must not offer to me animals like that which you have bought from a foreign person. I will not accept them as a gift of food from you. Those animals have bodies which are spoiled. They are not perfect.” ’
The Lord said to Moses,
‘When a calf, a lamb or a goat is born, it must stay with its mother for seven days. From the eighth day, you may offer it as a gift to the Lord. I will accept it.
You must not kill a cow or a sheep on the same day that you kill its baby.
When you bring an animal as a sacrifice to thank the Lord for something, offer it in the proper way. Then I will accept it as your gift.
You must eat the animal's meat on the same day that you offer it. None of the meat may remain until the next morning. I am the Lord.
You must be careful to obey my commands. I am the Lord.
You must not bring shame to my holy name. You people of Israel must know that I am holy as I live among you. I am the Lord and I cause you to be holy.
I am the one who brought you out of Egypt, so that I would be your God. I am the Lord.’
23: The Lord said to Moses,
‘Tell this to Israel's people: These are the special days and festivals that I have chosen. At these times you must meet together to worship me.

In each week, there are six days when you can do your work. The seventh day is the Sabbath day. On that day you must rest. You must not do any work. It is a holy day when you meet together to worship the Lord. This is the rule in all the places where you live.
There are also times that I have chosen for festivals. They are holy days when you must meet together to worship me.
The Passover festival will be on the 14th day of the first month of each year. It will begin when the sun goes down on that day. It is a time to give honour to the Lord.
On the next day, the 15th day of the first month, the festival of Flat Bread will begin, to give honour to the Lord. For seven days you must eat bread that has no yeast in it.
On the first of those seven days, you must all meet together to worship the Lord. You must not do your usual work.
For seven days you must offer sacrifices as a gift to the Lord. On the seventh day you must meet together again to worship the Lord. You must not do your usual work on that day.’
The Lord said to Moses,
‘Tell Israel's people, “When you go into the land that I will give you, crops will grow there. When you bring home the crops at harvest time, you must take some of the first grain to the priest.
He will lift up the grain as a special offering to the Lord. The Lord will accept your gift. The priest will do this on the day after the Sabbath day.
On the same day, you must also bring a perfect lamb that is one year old. That will be a burnt offering to the Lord.

You must also bring two tenths of an ephah of the best flour that is mixed with olive oil. That will be your gift to the Lord. The smell of this offering will give pleasure to the Lord. You must also offer 1 litre of wine as a drink offering.

You must not eat any of the new grain until you have done this. You must not use the grain to make bread. You must not cook it. Wait until the day that you bring the offering to your God. Then you may eat the grain. This is a rule that will continue for ever, in all the places where you live.
Count seven weeks from the day when the priest has made this offering of grain.
You must count until the day after the seventh Sabbath day. That will be 50 days. Then you must bring another grain offering to the Lord.

Each family must bring from their homes two loaves of bread as a special gift to the Lord. You must use two tenths of an ephah of good flour and yeast to make the bread. This is a gift to the Lord from the first crops of your harvest.

As well as the bread, the whole group of people must bring seven perfect lambs that are one year old. They must bring a young bull and two male sheep. These animals will be a burnt offering to the Lord, together with the grain offering and the drink offerings. The smell of these offerings while they are burning will give pleasure to the Lord.
You must also offer one male goat as a sin offering. And you must offer two lambs that are one year old as a friendship offering.
The priest will lift up the two lambs and the bread as a special gift to the Lord. They are a holy offering to the Lord that belongs to the priests.
On that day, you must not do any of your usual work. It is a day when you must meet together to worship the Lord. This is a rule that will continue for ever, in all the places where you live.

At harvest time, when you cut the crops on your land, do not cut to the edge of your field. Do not pick up the grain that falls onto the ground. Leave some of the grain for the poor people and for the foreign people who live among you. I am the Lord your God.” ’
The Lord said to Moses,
‘Tell Israel's people, “Each year, on the first day of the seventh month, you must have a complete rest. When you hear the sound of trumpets you must meet together to worship the Lord.
You must not do any of your usual work on that day. Instead, you must bring special offerings to the Lord.” ’
The Lord said to Moses,
‘The tenth day of the seventh month is the Day of Atonement. On that day, you must come together to worship the Lord. You must not eat any food. Instead, you must make a special offering to the Lord.
You must not do any work on that day. It is a day of atonement. The priests will offer sacrifices to the Lord your God to make you clean from your sins.
You must punish anyone who does not obey the rules for this day. That person can no longer belong among God's people.
I will destroy any person from among my people who does any work on that day.
You must not do any kind of work. This rule will continue for ever in all the places where you live.
It is a Sabbath day of complete rest for all of you. It is a day when you must not eat any food. You must rest from the evening of the ninth day until the evening of the tenth day.’
The Lord said to Moses,
‘Tell Israel's people, “Each year, on the 15th day of the seventh month, the Festival of Huts will start. The festival will continue for seven days, to give honour to the Lord.
On the first day of the festival, you must meet together to worship the Lord. You must not do any of your usual work.

For seven days you must offer special gifts to the Lord. On the eighth day you must meet together to worship the Lord. You must offer a special gift to him. It is the last day of the festival and you must all meet together. You must not do any of your usual work.

These are the rules for the special days that the Lord has chosen. They are times when you must meet together to worship the Lord. You will also offer special gifts to him. You will bring burnt offerings, grain offerings, drink offerings and sacrifices each day, as the rules teach.

You must meet to worship the Lord on these festival days, as well as on each Sabbath day. You must make these special offerings at festivals, as well as your usual gifts. You must give them to the Lord, as well as the gifts that you have promised to give and the gifts that you choose to give.

On the 15th day of the seventh month, you must begin the Festival of Huts. That is the time when you bring your crops home from the fields. This festival will continue for seven days, to give honour to the Lord. The first day and the eighth day of the festival are days of complete rest from your usual work.

On the first day, you must pick fruit from your best trees. Cut off branches of palm trees, willow trees and other trees with many leaves. Then for seven days you will be happy as you give honour to the Lord.
You must worship the Lord in this way for seven days. Do this in the seventh month of each year. This is a rule that will continue for all time.
All of you who were born as Israelites must live in huts for seven days.
Then your descendants in the future will know that Israel's people lived in tents for many years. I caused them to do that after I rescued them from the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.” ’
Moses told the Israelites these rules about the festivals that the Lord had chosen. They were times for them to worship him.
24: The Lord said to Moses,
‘ Command Israel's people to bring you pure oil from olives that they have beaten. Use this oil to burn in the lamps in the Tent of Meeting. This will cause the light to shine all the time.

Aaron must take care of the lamps that are in front of the curtain in the tent. The Covenant Box stands behind the curtain. Aaron must make sure that the lamps burn all night in front of the Lord, from evening until morning. This rule will continue for ever.
Aaron must take care of the lamps on the lampstand that is made of pure gold. He must make sure that the lamps always burn there, in front of the Lord.
Use the best flour to bake 12 round loaves of bread. Use two tenths of an ephah of flour for each loaf.
Put the loaves on the gold table in front of the Lord. Put them in two rows, with six loaves in each row.
Put pure frankincense along each row. This incense will burn as a special gift to the Lord instead of the bread.
Aaron must put new bread on the table every Sabbath day. It shows the covenant that continues for ever between Israel's people and the Lord.
The bread will belong to Aaron and his sons. They must eat it in a holy place because it is a very holy offering to the Lord. It will always be a part of the offerings that belongs to the priests.’

There was a man whose mother's name was Shelomith. She was an Israelite woman, the daughter of Dibri who belonged to the tribe of Dan. The man's father was an Egyptian. The man started to fight against an Israelite man inside the camp. While they were fighting, the man insulted the Lord 's name with a curse.
So the Israelites took hold of the man and they put guards to watch him. They wanted the Lord to show them what they should do.
The Lord said to Moses,
‘Take the man who spoke the curse outside the camp. All the people who heard what he said must put their hands on his head. Then all the people must throw stones at him to kill him.
Warn Israel's people, “If anyone curses me, their God, they deserve their punishment.

So if someone insults the Lord 's name, his punishment must be death. All the people must throw stones at him until he is dead. It does not matter whether he is a foreign person or an Israelite. You must kill anyone who insults the Lord 's name.
If anyone kills another person, you must punish him with death.
If someone kills an animal that belongs to someone else, he must pay for it with a living animal. He took away a life, so he must give a life.
If a man hurts another person, you must punish him as he deserves. Hurt him in the same way that he has hurt the other person.
If he has broken one of the person's bones, break one of his bones. If he hurt the person's eye, hurt his eye. If he has broken a person's tooth, break his tooth.
If someone kills an animal that belongs to someone else, he must pay for it with a living animal. But if someone kills another person, you must punish him with death.
The same rule is for everyone, both Israelites and foreign people who live among you. I am the Lord your God.” ’

Moses told Israel's people what the Lord had said. They took the man who spoke the curse outside the camp. They threw stones at him until he died. Israel's people did what the Lord had commanded Moses to do.
25: When Moses was on Mount Sinai, the Lord said to him,
‘Tell Israel's people this: You will go into the land that I will give to you. Then the land must have a rest every seven years. That will give me honour.
For six years, you may plant seeds in your fields. You may take care of your vines. You may bring home your crops at harvest time.
But in the seventh year, the land must have a complete rest. It is a Sabbath year to give honour to the Lord. You must not plant seeds in your fields. You must not take care of your vines.
You must not cut your crops or your grapes at harvest time. The land must have a year of complete rest.

But some things will still grow on the land during that year. You may eat anything that grows by itself in the Sabbath year. That will be food for you and for your servants. It will be food for the people that you pay to work for you. And it will be food for the foreign people who are living with you.
The land will also provide food for your farm animals and for the wild animals. You may eat any food that the land provides during that year.
You must also count seven Sabbath years. That is seven times seven years, or 49 years.
In the 50th year you must make a loud noise with trumpets everywhere in your land. Do that on the tenth day of the seventh month, the Day of Atonement.

You must make sure that the 50th year is a special holy year. It is a happy Jubilee year. It is a time for everyone in the land to become free from their debts. Anyone who has sold his family's land will receive it back again. Slaves will return to live with their own people.
The 50th year is a Jubilee year for you. You must not plant any seeds in your fields. You must not cut your crops at harvest time. You must not pick any grapes from your vines.
It is a Jubilee year for you. You must keep it as a holy year. You may only eat food that grows by itself in your fields.
In the Jubilee year you will all return to the land that belongs to your family.
At any time, if you agree with someone to buy or sell land, you must be fair.

If you buy some land, you must count the number of years until the next Jubilee year. That will decide the fair price for you to pay. You are paying for the number of years that you can grow crops on the land.

If there are many years until the Jubilee year, the price of the land will be larger. If only a few years remain, the price will be smaller. The other man is selling you a certain number of years when you will get crops from the land.
Do not cheat each other. Respect and obey your God. I am the Lord your God.
You must obey my rules and my laws. If you are careful to do that, you will live safely in the land.
The land will provide plenty of food for you to eat. You will live there safely.
You may think, “If we do not plant any seeds in the seventh year, what food will we eat?”
This is what will happen. I will bless the land in the sixth year so that it grows a lot of food for you. It will provide enough crops to give you food for three years!

When you plant seeds in the eighth year, you will still be eating your old crops. You will be eating food that the land provided in the sixth year. You will continue to eat those old crops until you bring home new crops in the ninth year.
You must never sell your land so that it completely belongs to someone else. The land always belongs to me, the Lord. I allow you to live on it for a time, like foreigners.
Anybody who sells land must always have the chance to buy it back again.
If an Israelite becomes poor, he may need to sell some of his land. If so, the person who has authority in his family must buy the land back from the new owner.
There might not be anyone in the man's family who can do that. Later, the man himself may have enough money to buy the land back again.
He must count how many years remain until the next Jubilee year. He must pay the value of those years to the man that he sold the land to. Then it will become his own land again.

But the man may never have enough money to pay the value of the land. If so, the land that he sold will belong to the other person until the next Jubilee year. When the Jubilee year comes, the new owner must return the land to the poor man who sold it. Then the man can return to live on his land again.

It is different for a house inside a city that has walls. If someone sells his house in a city, he may buy it back in the first year. For one complete year after he has sold it, he has the chance to buy it back.

If the man does not buy back the house in that year, it will completely belong to the new owner. It will continue to belong to him and to his descendants for ever. They will not have to return it in the Jubilee year.

But a house may be in a village that has no walls around it. If someone sells the house, it is the same as a piece of land like a field. The person who sells it must always have the chance to buy it back again. If not, when the Jubilee year comes, the house will belong again to the first owner.
Some cities belong to the Levites for them to live in. If a Levite sells his house, he must always have the chance to buy it back again.

When the Jubilee year comes, a house that a Levite has sold will belong to him again. This is because the Levites need to live in the houses that belong to them in their cities. The other Israelite tribes have given those places to the Levites.
But the Levites must never sell the fields that are around their cities. That land belongs to them for ever.
If an Israelite becomes poor, he may not be able to take care of himself. You must help him with the things that he needs. Help him to live with you, as you would help a foreigner or a stranger.
If you lend money to him, do not take back more than you gave him. Respect and obey your God. Allow the poor man to live with you as part of your family.
If you lend money to him, or you sell food to him, do not take more than its true value.
I am the Lord your God who rescued you from the land of Egypt. Now I am giving you the land of Canaan. I have done this to be your God.
If a poor Israelite is in debt to you, he might sell himself to you. But you must not make him work for you as a slave.
Instead, you must pay him to work as your servant, or like a foreigner who is living with you. He must work for you until the next Jubilee year.
In the Jubilee year, he will be free to leave. He and his children can go back to his own family on the land that belongs to his ancestors.
Israel's people are my servants. I brought them out of Egypt. So nobody may buy them as slaves.
You must not be cruel to poor people. Instead, respect and obey your God.
You are allowed to buy male and female slaves, if they are people from other nations.
You may also buy people from the families of foreign people who live among you. The children that they give birth to in your land may also become your slaves.
Foreign slaves may continue to belong to you for your whole life. After you die, they can belong to your children. But you must not make other Israelites your slaves. You must not be cruel to them.
A foreign person who lives with you might become rich. A poor Israelite might be in debt to him. So the Israelite might sell himself to the foreigner or to someone from the foreigner's family.
Someone from the family of the poor Israelite always has the chance to buy him back.
It may be his brother, his uncle or his cousin. Anyone from his own family may buy him back so that he is free again. And if the poor man himself has success, he can pay to make himself free.

The poor man and the foreign man who bought him must decide together the fair price. They must count the number of years that remain until the next Jubilee year. They must decide how much pay a worker would receive during that time. That is the right price for the poor Israelite man to become free.
Many years may remain until the Jubilee year. If so, the man must pay most of the sale money to be free.
If only a few years remain, the man will pay a smaller price to become free.
The foreign person who has bought the Israelite man must pay him each year, as he would pay a worker. Do not allow the foreign owner to be cruel to the Israelite.
When the Jubilee year comes, the poor Israelite man must go free, if he has not already become free. His children will go free with him.
Israel's people are my own servants. They belong to me because I rescued them from the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.’
26: The Lord said to Moses, ‘You must not make idols for yourselves. Do not use wood or stone to make images or statues of false gods. Do not worship any stone shape that you have put on your land. I am the Lord your God.
You must obey my rules about Sabbath days. You must respect my holy tabernacle. I am the Lord.
You must carefully obey my laws and my commands.
If you do, I will send rain at the proper time each year. The land will provide you with food. Plenty of fruit will grow on your trees.

You will continue to bring in grain from your fields until it is time to pick your grapes. And you will continue to pick your grapes until it is time to plant seeds for the next year. You will have all the food to eat that you need. You will live safely in your land.
I will cause there to be peace in your land. You will not be afraid of trouble when you lie down to sleep. I will remove dangerous animals from your land. No enemies will come to attack you.
You will be able to chase after your enemies and kill them.
Five of your men will chase away 100 of your enemies. 100 of your men will chase away 10,000 enemies. You will attack them and kill them.
I will help you. I will cause you to have many children so that the number of your people grows. I will do what I have promised to do in my covenant with you.
When harvest time comes each year, you will still be eating food that you have stored. You will need to throw food out of your store rooms to make space for the new crops.
I will have my home among you. I will never turn against you.
I will walk among you. I will be your God and you will be my people.
I am the Lord your God. I brought you out from Egypt so that you would no longer be slaves of the Egyptians. I destroyed the power that caused you to suffer. Then you could live as free people.
But it will be different if you do not respect me and obey my commands.
I will punish you if you do not accept my laws and you turn against my rules. I will punish you if you do not obey all my commands and you spoil the covenant that I made with you.

This is how I will punish you: I will cause terrible things to happen to you. You will have diseases that make you weak and diseases that make your bodies very hot. You will have diseases that spoil your eyes and diseases that take away your strength. You will plant seeds in your fields but your enemies will eat the crops.
I will turn against you. Your enemies will attack you and they will win against you. People who hate you will rule over you. You will run away to escape even if nobody is chasing you!
Even after I have punished you like that, you might still choose not to obey me. If so, I will continue to punish you seven times more for your sins.
I will stop you being proud of your strength. I will not send any rain from the sky. Your land will become hard and dry.
You will use all your strength to work very hard, but it will be useless. Your land will not provide any food for you. No fruit will grow on your trees.
If you continue to turn against me, I will cause you to suffer even more. If you refuse to obey me, I will punish you seven times more again for your sins.
I will send wild animals to attack you. They will kill your children and your animals. Very few of your people will remain and nobody will travel on your roads.
After all that punishment, you may still not turn back to me. If you continue to turn against me,
I myself will turn against you. I will punish you another seven times more strongly for your sins.

I will send armies to attack you. They will punish you because you have not obeyed my covenant with you. If you escape into your cities, I will send bad diseases among you. Then your enemies will have power over you.
I will stop you getting any food. Ten women will bake all the bread for their families in only one oven. They will weigh the bread for each person but nobody will have enough to eat.
After this, you may still not obey me. If you continue to turn against me,
I will be angry and I will turn against you. I myself will punish you seven times more strongly because of your sins.
Then you will be so hungry that you will eat your own children.
I will destroy your altars for sacrifices to false gods and your altars for incense. I will put your dead bodies in a heap on top of your dead idols. I will hate you.
I will destroy your cities and the places where you worship. The sweet smell of your offerings will not give me pleasure.
I myself will destroy the land where you live. Your enemies who come to live there will be very upset when they see it.
I will chase you out to go and live among other nations. I will cause enemies to attack you. I will destroy your land. Your cities will become heaps of stones.
While you are living in the countries of your enemies, your own land will have a rest. It will have the Sabbath rest that it should have had every seven years.
When you lived on the land, you did not allow it to have its Sabbath rest. Now it will have complete rest while it is an empty place.

Some of you will remain alive in the countries of your enemies. But you will live in great fear. The sound of a leaf that moves in the wind will cause you to run away. You will think that someone is chasing you to kill you. Nobody is chasing you, but you will still fall down as you try to escape.
You will fall over each other, like people who are running away from their enemies. Nobody is chasing you, but you are still afraid. None of you will be strong enough to fight against your enemies.
You will die among the nations where you are living. That is where you will come to an end.
Those of you who still remain will slowly die in the countries of your enemies. You will become weak and you will die because of your sins and because of the sins of your ancestors.

But one day, your descendants may realize that they and their ancestors have been wrong. They may clearly agree that they have turned against me. They may agree that they have not been faithful to me.

That was why I myself turned against them and I took them to the countries of their enemies. When they know that, they will no longer be proud. They will accept the punishment that they deserve for their sins.
When that happens, I will do what I have promised to do. I made those promises in my covenant with your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac and Jacob. I will remember to bless the land that I gave to them.

It was necessary for my people to go away from the land for a time. The land needed to be empty so that it could have the Sabbath rest that it should have had. My people refused to obey my rules. They hated my laws. So they needed to receive the punishment that they deserved for their sins.

But I am the Lord their God. So I will not forget them while they are living in the countries of their enemies. I will not turn away from them. I will not destroy them completely. I will not forget the covenant that I made with them.
Yes, I will help them because of the covenant that I made with their ancestors. While other nations watched, I brought my people out of Egypt to be their God. I am the Lord.’
These are the laws, rules and commands that the Lord told Moses on Mount Sinai. They are his covenant with Israel's people.
27: The Lord said to Moses,
‘Speak to Israel's people. Tell them, “Someone might promise to offer another person as a gift to the Lord. He may pay money as his offering instead of the person.
He must pay the value of the offering that he has promised. For a man between 20 years and 60 years old, the value is 50 shekels of silver that they use in the holy place.
For a woman, the value is 30 shekels of silver.
For a male between 5 years and 20 years old, the value is 20 shekels. For a female of that age, the value is 10 shekels.
For a boy between one month and five years old, the value is 5 shekels. For a girl of the same age, the value is 3 shekels.
For a man who is older than 60 years, the value is 15 shekels. For a woman of that age, the value is 10 shekels.

The person who makes the promise might be too poor to pay the value of his offering. If so, he must bring the person that he has offered to God to the priest. The priest will decide the value of the poor person's offering. He will decide how much money the poor person is able to give.
Someone might promise to offer an animal as a gift to the Lord. If it is the kind of animal that the Lord would accept as an offering, the animal becomes a holy offering.

The person must not then change his offering in any way. He must not change a good animal for a bad animal. He must not change a bad animal for a good animal. If the person does change one animal for another, both the animals become holy.
The animal that the person promises to give might be an unclean animal. The Lord does not accept that kind of animal as an offering. So the person must bring the animal to the priest.
The priest will look at the animal to see whether it is good or bad. Then he will decide its value as an offering. The priest is the person who decides the value.
The person who promised his animal as an offering might want to buy the animal back. If so, he must pay the value of the animal and add one fifth more.
Someone might offer his house as a holy gift to the Lord. The priest will look at the house to see whether it is good or bad. Then he will decide its value as an offering.
If the person who offered the house wants to buy it back, he must pay its value and add one fifth more. Then the house will belong to him again.

Someone might offer part of his family's land as a holy gift to the Lord. He must decide how much seed the land needs to grow crops. The value of the land will be 50 shekels of silver for each homer of seed that the land needs.
If a person gives his land to the Lord in a Jubilee year, the land will have its complete value.

But the land's value will be less if the person gives it after the Jubilee year. The priest will count the number of years that remain until the next Jubilee. Then he will decide the value of the land.
If the person wants to buy the land back, he must pay its value and add one fifth more. Then the land will belong to him again.
It is different if he does not buy the land back and he sells it to someone else. If he does that, he is not allowed to buy the land back for himself.
When the next Jubilee year comes, the land will become the Lord 's holy land for ever. It will belong to the priests.
Someone might offer to the Lord a field that is not part of his family's land. It might be a field that he has bought from someone.

If so, the priest will count how many years remain until the Jubilee year. Then he will decide the value of the field. The person who offered the field to the Lord must pay the money on that day. The money belongs to the Lord as a holy gift.
When the Jubilee year comes, the field will belong again to the first owner. It is part of his family's land.
The priest is the person who must decide the value of all these offerings. He must use the shekel that they use in the holy place to decide the value. There are 20 gerahs in one shekel.
The first baby that an animal gives birth to is holy. It belongs to the Lord. So nobody can offer it as a special gift to the Lord. If it is an ox or a sheep, it already belongs to the Lord.
But if it is an unclean animal, the owner may pay to buy it. He must pay the value and add one fifth more. If the owner does not buy it, the priest must sell it to someone else for its value.

Someone might give to the Lord as a complete gift something that belongs to him. It might be a person, an animal or part of his family's land. Every gift like that belongs to the Lord for ever as a most holy gift. The priest must not sell it. The owner cannot ever buy it back.
If you give a person to the Lord as a complete gift, nobody may buy him back. His punishment must be death.
One tenth of all the food that the land provides belongs to the Lord as a holy gift. It may be grain that grows in the fields, or fruit that grows on the trees.
If a person chooses to buy back any of his tithe, he must pay its value and add one fifth more.
Out of every ten farm animals, one animal is holy. It belongs to the Lord.

The owner must not pick what animal he will give to the Lord. He must not change a good animal for a bad animal. He must not change a bad animal for a good animal. If anyone does that, both animals will belong to the Lord as holy animals. The owner cannot buy the animal back for himself.” ’
These are the commands that the Lord gave to Moses on Mount Sinai. He told Moses to teach them to Israel's people.
